channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: South and Midwest face potentially catastrophic rains and floods while reeling from tornadoes
item.description: Parts of the Midwest and South are facing the possibility torrential rains and life-threatening flash floods on Friday The fresh storms come as many communities are still reeling from severe tornadoes that destroyed whole neighborhoods and killed at least seven people.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Sainz, George Walker Iv And John Seewer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Colleges say the Trump administration is using new tactics to expel international students
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A crackdown on foreign students is alarming colleges, who say the Trump administration is using new tactics and vague justifications to push some students out of the country. College officials worry the new approach will keep foreigners from wanting to study in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, ANNIE MA and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump administration fires staff of program that helps low-income households pay for heat
item.description: The Trump administration has laid off the entire staff of a $4.1 billion program that helps low-income households nationwide pay for heat.
item.dc:creator: Susan Haigh, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mexico to dump millions of gallons of sewage into Tijuana River, US continues to be a dumping ground: Official
item.description: Mexico will dump 400 million gallons of sewage into the Tijuana River, which could spill into the united States, a San Diego official said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: K-12 schools must sign certification against DEI to receive federal money, administration says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As a condition for receiving federal money, the Trump administration is ordering K-12 schools to certify that they are following federal civil rights laws and ending any discriminatory diversity, equity and inclusion practices.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ford offering employee pricing to all shoppers in wake of Trump tariffs
item.description: The campaign comes as Trump has rolled out a fresh package of tariffs, impacting nearly every country in the world.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Remains of Illinois woman who disappeared in 1983 found after cold case investigators start podcast
item.description: Karen Schepers' remains were finally identified after being found in a car submerged in the Fox River, nearly 43 years after she went missing, thanks to the efforts of the Elgin Police Department and the non-profit organization Chaos Divers.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, KRON4

item.title: Snatched pets and livestock deaths blamed on wolves prompt emergency in rural New Mexico
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Commissioners in a rural New Mexico county say pets are being snatched from front yards and livestock are being maimed and killed by endangered Mexican gray wolves that seem to have no fear of humans, prompting them to declare a state of emergency.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What you do before and during a tornado could mean the difference between life and death
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — With new innovations and technology, scientists have learned from major tornado outbreaks over the years how to improve safety guidance. During tornado season, forecasters try to get the word out about staying safe during a storm — but also planning for it ahead of time.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: ICE releases man seeking life-saving transplant for brother after a month in custody
item.description: Jose Gregorio Gonzalez had been detained by ICE after he accompanied his brother, Jose Alfredo Gonzalez, to a kidney dialysis appointment.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, KRON4

item.title: Puppy beaten outside Memphis restaurant has broken bones, human bite mark
item.description: A day after the video surfaced, the dog was surrendered to police, and a warrant was issued for aggravated animal cruelty.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Moon, NewsNation

item.title: 30+ dogs, including 19 doodles, rescued from Texas home
item.description: The dogs, which were between 3 months and 5 years old, were living outside “as part of a breeding operation of ‘doodle’ type dogs.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Russ, NewsNation

item.title: Tariffs hit tiny coral islands in the South Pacific and Arctic outposts
item.description: The Trump administration appears to be leaving no stone unturned with its sweeping tariffs around the globe, from rocky outcrops home to polar bears in the Arctic to tiny tropical islands.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: American Airlines passengers evacuate flight on wing of plane after landing in Georgia, video shows
item.description: Passengers onboard an American Airlines plane evacuated the aircraft from its wings after smoke was reported in the cabin upon landing in Augusta, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Markets plunge in reaction to Trump 'Liberation Day' tariffs
item.description: Stocks plunged Thursday morning as U.S. trading opened for the first time after President Trump’s announcement of heavy tariffs on nearly every nation exporting products to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KTLA 5

item.title: This is what forecasters mean when they talk about a 100-year flood
item.description: Weather forecasters sometimes warn of storms that unleash rains so unusual they are described as 100-year or even 500-year floods. Here’s what to know about how scientists determine how extreme a flood is and how common these extreme events are becoming.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams will skip the Democratic primary and run for reelection as an independent
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams says he will pull out of the city’s Democratic primary and will instead run for reelection as an independent candidate.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump gambles on tariffs as nation braces for economic fallout
item.description: President Trump took his biggest gamble yet on the economy Wednesday when he pressed forward with sweeping tariffs on imports from nearly every country in the world.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Storms kill 7 in the South and Midwest as forecasters warn of catastrophic rains, floods this week
item.description: Violent storms and tornadoes have torn through cities from Oklahoma to Indiana during what could be a record period of deadly weather and flooding.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Martin And Hallie Golden, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 1 person dead as violent storms, including tornadoes, wallop Midwest and South
item.description: Tornadoes and violent storms struck parts of the South and Midwest on Wednesday, killing at least one person, knocking down power lines and trees, ripping roofs off homes and shooting debris thousands of feet into the air.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New bill seeks to alleviate burden on Florida citrus growers as orange groves disappear
item.description: As Florida citrus growers continue to face hardships, causing some to make the difficult decision to sell their orange groves, a new piece of bipartisan legislation is aiming to alleviate some of the burden.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Amazon's last-minute bid for TikTok comes as a US ban on the platform is set to take effect Saturday
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Amazon has put in a bid to purchase TikTok, a Trump administration official said Wednesday, in an eleventh-hour pitch as a U.S. ban on the platform is set to go into effect Saturday.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New documents describe physical condition of man allegedly held captive for decades in Connecticut
item.description: An investigator said the man's skin appeared "to be graying," and that his physical condition resembled someone "at the end of their life.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, Fox 5

item.title: Jackie and Shadow's bald eagle chicks officially have names - Sunny and Gizmo
item.description: The baby eagles in California's Big Bear Valley that have captured the hearts and minds of people around the world finally have names.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Musk could be headed for a Washington exit after turbulent times at DOGE
item.description: President Trump has said that DOGE will end at some point and Musk will return to running his companies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: HHS fires entire staff of program that helps low-income people afford heat and air conditioning
item.description: The Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) has fired all of the workers in its program that seeks to help low-income Americans pay their energy bills.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9

item.title: No bathroom breaks: How Booker accomplished record Senate speech
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) set a new record for the longest Senate speech in history on Monday and Tuesday by holding the upper chamber’s floor for more than 25 hours.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chocolate bars recalled over possible small stones
item.description: Dutch chocolate maker Tony's Chocoloney has issued a nationwide recall of two of its chocolate products, which may contain small stones.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: Judge holds ICE agent in contempt after he detained suspect during a trial
item.description: ICE agent Brian Sullivan detained Wilson Martell-Lebron last week as he was leaving court. But a Boston Municipal Court judge issued a ruling Monday against Sullivan, arguing that he had deprived Martell-Lebron of his rights.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, WGN 9

item.title: 4 key takeaways as Trump’s sweeping HHS layoffs begin
item.description: Thousands of employees across the vast Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) began receiving layoff notices as early as 5 a.m. on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: April Fool's prank renames French Quarter to American Quarter in New Orleans
item.description: An April Fool’s prank left many New Orleans residents and visitors stunned after a fake website announced that the historic French Quarter had been renamed the “American Quarter” by executive order of Louisiana Gov. Jeff Landry.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Spears, Fox 5

item.title: NFL to use camera technology to measure first downs next season, approves more rule changes
item.description: The NFL announced a multitude of rule changes on Tuesday, including a new way to measure first downs for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Jared Bush, Fox 5

item.title: McMahon meeting with House Democrats over Education Department layoffs
item.description: Education Secretary Linda McMahon is having a meeting with House Democrats on Wednesday over their concerns surrounding the layoffs at the Department of Education, which she and President Trump have been vocal about trying to eliminate entirely. Rep. Mark Takano (Calif.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man called 'Scamdaddy' sentenced for defrauding Utah-based credit unions out of $450K
item.description: A man who called himself "Scamdaddy" was sentenced for his involvement in a scheme to defraud Utah credit unions out of more than $450,000.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Social Security Administration probes origin of website portal outage
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Social Security Administration said Tuesday that it is investigating the root cause of website outages that have affected the “my Social Security” portal where recipients access their benefits.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fire kills New York cat sanctuary founder and dozens of animals he rescued
item.description: A fire burned down a Long Island cat shelter, killing its founder, who lived there, and at least 59 of the felines he rescued, authorities said. The fire at the Happy Cat Sanctuary in the hamlet of Medford was reported shortly after 7 a.m. Monday. The cause is under investigation.
item.dc:creator: JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump is stronger on immigration and weaker on trade, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Immigration remains a strength for President Donald Trump, but his handling of tariffs is getting more negative feedback, according to a poll from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research. About half of U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4
